ref: One day, Abraham called in his most trusted servant and said to him, “Solemnly promise me
text: Na Abraham sele ye somvoɛ panyi paa bɔ ɔwɔ ye awuro berɛ, na osa nea ye nningyein mukoraa so ne kyɛ, “Fa wɔ sa fua me sɔ nu,
translated: And Abraham was now very old, and the Lord had blessed him in all things. So he said to the oldest servant in his house,

ref: He told his servants, “Stay here with the donkey, while my son and I go over there to worship. We will come back.”
text: Na Abraham sele ye somvoɛ nemɔ kyɛ, “Ɛmɔ ne afunum ne bɛtena wa; na me ne akwadaa ye yɛkwaasɔne Nyameɛ wɔ moa dɔ, na yɛkosa yɛkɔwa ɛmɔ nwo wa.”
translated: Then Abraham fell on his face and laughed, and said in his heart, “Shall a child be born to a man who is a hundred years old? Shall Sarah, who is ninety years old, bear a child?”

ref: The rainbow that I have put in the sky will be my sign to you and to every living creature on earth. It will remind you that I will keep this promise forever.
text: Mafa me nyangondoen mabea munungum nu, na ɔkɔyɛ apam bɔ me ne aseɛ yayɛ ne nwo sɛngyerɛnneɛ.
translated: The child was very happy when he saw the dog, and he ran to his mother to tell her about the beautiful animal.

ref: Ezer's sons were Bilhan, Zaavan, and Akan.
text: Esere mma mmienzua mɔ ɛhe: Bilhan, Saawan na Akan.
translated: The three children of Esere are: Bilhan, Saawan, and Akan.

ref: Anah's children were Dishon and Oholibamah.

translated: And his mother said to him: Dison, you are Ana's brother, the son of Oholibama.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.

translated: God created the world and everything in it, God's creation is good and beautiful; it consists of two parts, the sky and the earth.

ref: Take seven pairs of every kind of animal that can be used for sacrifice and one pair of all others.

translated: If you don't know how to do it, don't do it at all; if you know how to do it, do it well.

translated: If you see me, when I am dead and my corpse is being carried, don’t cry for me, just dance and rejoice.

text: Ɔfale mmakaa bɔ wawɔngyewɔngye bɛ nwo ne guale nningyein bɔ ngaka nemɔ no nu nzue ne anwo. Né sɛ ngaka nemɔ ba kyɛ bɛbaano nzue a, berɛ yeɛ bɛfo bɛ nwo ɔ.
translated: The elders say that if a child does not listen to advice and continues to disobey, when the consequences come, they will suffer alone.

ref: Jacob said, “Now tell me your name.” “Don't you know who I am?” he asked. And he blessed Jacob.
text: Yeɛ Yakob sele ye kyɛ, “Mesrɛ wɔ, bɔ wɔ dumaa kyire me.” Na Biaa ne buale kyɛ, “Nzu ati yeɛ ebisa me dumaa ɔ?” Na oyirale Yakob wɔ berɛ.
translated: Jacob said to him, “Tell me your name.” But he replied, “Why do you ask my name?” Then he blessed Jacob there.

ref: Some of the sheep that Jacob was keeping for Laban were already spotted. And when the others were ready to mate, he made sure that they faced in the direction of the spotted and black ones. In this way, Jacob built up a flock of sheep for himself and did not put them with the other sheep.
text: Yakob yeyele mmoaen mmaa nemɔ siele ahameɛ, ɔmane bɛ ngaa ne fale bɛ nye hyirele Laban mmoaen bɔ bɛ nwo te krukyerɛɛ ɔne bɛ bɔ bɛ nwo te bire ne amukoraa. Yakob fale atee he aso siele ye ngaka deɛ mɔ wɔ nekaa foforɛ bɔ wamva bɛ wamvora Laban deɛ nu ɔ.
translated: Jacob was a man who had many wives and children, but he did not have peace in his house because his wives were always quarreling with each other. Jacob's wives were jealous of each other, and they were always fighting over who was more loved by him. Jacob's wives did not want to stay in Laban's house anymore because of the constant quarrels.

ref: When the man saw that he could not win, he struck Jacob on the hip and threw it out of joint.
text: Na biaa ne nwuni ye kyɛ ɔngora nni Yakob so ngonim ne, ɔbɔle Yakob taku so, na Yakob dwongu fandili wɔ ngɔtɔfaen ne anu.
translated: And the man saw that he could not prevail against Jacob, so he touched Jacob's hip socket, and Jacob's hip was put out of joint as he wrestled with him.

ref: The Lord knew that Jacob loved Rachel more than he did Leah, and so he gave children to Leah, but not to Rachel.
text: AWURADE nwuni kyɛ Yakob nguro Lea, yiti ɔmane Lea wole mmaa, nakoso Rahɛl de ɔyɛle mmotaen.
translated: The Lord saw that Leah was hated, so he opened her womb, but Rachel was barren.

ref: Take Rebekah with you; she can marry your master's son, just as the Lord has said.”

translated: Now, Rebecca is old and advanced in years. It is time for us to find a wife for our son Isaac, so that the Lord may be pleased.
